raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "Maps against the nearest 3-5 works; identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "R1.7": "Flags one missing citation.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers a substantially different active set than requested.",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "issues_to_address": [
    "The 'central limitation' claim is too strong as stated.",
    "The three-thread taxonomy needs a citation sweep.",
    "The novelty claim needs hedging or a sharper definition.",
    "The 23% number needs more scaffolding even in the introduction.",
    "The mechanism paragraph asserts three failure modes without supporting them.",
    "We believe this design principle generalizes beyond code synthesis is unsupported in the body.",
    "Minor language and consistency edits.",
    "Structural suggestion.",
    "Forward references.",
    "Potential framing risk to watch."
  ],
  "items_i_could_not_verify": [
    "Citation years and authorship for CAMEL, Generative Agents, ReAct, and HuggingGPT are stated from memory of common usage and not checked against the actual references; please confirm against your bibliography.",
    "I did not verify the 23-point empirical claim, the benchmark, or the identity of the 'strongest prior baseline.' The recommendations above assume these are correct as stated by the author.",
    "The novelty assessment in item 3 depends on the precise wording of MetaGPT's and AutoGen's handoff mechanisms; the safe move is for you to re-read those papers' communication-protocol sections before locking in the 'first to' claim, as my characterization is from general familiarity rather than a fresh reading.",
    "The taxonomy gaps flagged in item 2 are based on what is conventionally cited in this area as of my training; if your venue or subfield has different conventions, weight accordingly."
  ]
}
